criteria.name: seeded_library_and_tree
criteria.description: On first load the sidebar lists at least 4 seeded schemas (an evaluation result, an agent task, prompt metadata, and a classification response), the first is active and marked, and its tree renders at least 6 fields including at least one nested object and one array, each row showing key name, a type badge, and a required toggle, with chevrons collapsing object and array children

criteria.name: add_field_and_inline_rename
criteria.description: Clicking Add Field on an object node appends a child with a default name and string type; clicking a node's name makes it editable inline and committing the edit renames it in the tree, the compiled text, and the example payload

criteria.name: delete_with_inline_confirm_root_protected
criteria.description: A node's Delete control shows an inline confirmation; confirming removes the node and all its descendants, canceling leaves it intact, and the root node offers no Delete control

criteria.name: drag_reorder_and_nesting
criteria.description: Dragging a node with the real pointer reorders it among its siblings immediately on drop, and the nest/un-nest controls move a node into the preceding object sibling or out to its parent's level, with the tree and compiled text updating immediately

criteria.name: bulk_node_actions
criteria.description: Multi-selecting nodes via checkboxes reveals a contextual action bar with Set required, Clear required, and Delete selected (its confirmation naming the count), and each action applies to exactly the selected nodes

criteria.name: config_panel_field_definition_contract
criteria.description: Clicking a node opens a side configuration panel that edits a FieldDefinition payload with key (1 to 40 characters, letters/digits/underscores, sibling-unique), type (closed enum string|number|boolean|object|array), required boolean, optional description, and type-dependent constraints — enumValues and pattern for string, minimum and maximum for number — and the visible constraint inputs change when the type changes

criteria.name: constraint_validation_blocks_invalid
criteria.description: Entering a minimum greater than the maximum, an invalid pattern expression, a sibling-duplicate or illegal key, or an empty enum entry shows an inline message naming the field and the FieldDefinition rule, and the invalid value is not applied to the compiled schema

criteria.name: constraint_templates_apply
criteria.description: The templates library lists at least Email pattern, Percentage 0 to 100, ISO date pattern, Non-empty string, and Status enum, and applying one fills the matching constraint inputs on the selected node with visible values that appear in the compiled text

criteria.name: compiled_text_live_and_faithful
criteria.description: The compiled JSON Schema text updates within roughly 100 milliseconds of any tree or configuration edit and is standard, consumable JSON Schema — valid JSON that parses, with a draft-07 $schema declaration and the standard type, properties, required, enum, minimum, maximum, and pattern keywords for the nodes defining them; renames and deletions change the text correspondingly, and its Copy control puts the exact text on the clipboard with visible confirmation

criteria.name: example_satisfies_schema
criteria.description: The Example tab shows a generated JSON object satisfying the current schema — enum fields use one of their enum values, numbers fall within bounds, and required fields are present — and the Regenerate control produces different randomized values that still satisfy the schema

criteria.name: example_tracks_every_edit
criteria.description: Adding, renaming, retyping, or constraining a field immediately changes the example payload correspondingly, and the example never shows a field the schema no longer contains

criteria.name: format_instruction_and_prompt_draft
criteria.description: The Format Prompt tab shows a natural-language instruction naming each field with its type and required status, updating when the schema changes, and the Insert into prompt draft control appends it to the editable prompt draft drawer with a success toast

criteria.name: validation_run_steps_visible
criteria.description: Run validation on a pasted payload starts a simulated run with one visible step per top-level schema field, each advancing through pending, running, and complete or failed as the run progresses

criteria.name: retry_backoff_and_resume_from_step
criteria.description: A step that retries shows a visible backoff countdown and attempt counter (such as retry 2 of 3); a step that exhausts retries is marked failed with an inline error summary and a manual Retry control that resumes the run from that step while completed steps keep their outcomes and never re-execute

criteria.name: pause_resume_rollup_timeline
criteria.description: Pausing a running validation freezes at the current step and resuming continues from exactly that step; a rollup of fields checked out of total and failure count derives live from the step states, and an ordered event timeline of step transitions with timestamps can be filtered by status

criteria.name: annotations_map_onto_tree
criteria.description: When the run completes, per-field pass/fail annotations appear on the tree — success markers on passing nodes and error markers with messages naming the field path and the violated constraint (for example, score must be at most 100) on failing nodes

criteria.name: invalid_json_blocks_run
criteria.description: Pasting a payload that is not valid JSON shows an inline parse error naming the position and does not start a validation run

criteria.name: import_inference_follows_example
criteria.description: Pasting a JSON object into Import from example produces an inferred schema draft whose field names, types, and constraints match the pasted object's keys and values, with per-field accept and reject toggles; pasting a different object produces a visibly different draft

criteria.name: apply_import_replaces_tree
criteria.description: Applying the reviewed import draft replaces the working tree with exactly the accepted fields — rejected fields are omitted — and the compiled text and example payload update to match

criteria.name: version_save_validation
criteria.description: Save version requires a name — saving with an empty name shows an inline message naming the field and saves nothing — and saved versions list with name and timestamp, newest first

criteria.name: structural_diff_color_coded
criteria.description: The Diff view compares two selected versions with added fields in a success treatment, removed fields in an error treatment, and changed fields (type, required, or constraints) in a warning treatment each labeled with what changed; changing a selection updates the diff without a reload and diffing a version against itself shows an explicit no-differences state

criteria.name: library_new_delete_duplicate
criteria.description: The sidebar's New button starts a blank schema with a root object, Delete removes the selected schema after confirmation, Duplicate copies it under a copy-suffixed name, and clicking any entry loads it into the tree with the active entry visually marked

criteria.name: metadata_field_builder
criteria.description: The metadata field builder accepts a field label (required) and a type (text, number, date, or dropdown with options); defined fields immediately render as editable inputs on every library entry's details, and values entered there display on that entry

criteria.name: undo_redo_with_labels
criteria.description: Undo and Redo revert and reapply adds, renames, retypes, constraint edits, reorders, nesting, deletions, bulk actions, and imports, each control showing the label of the action it will affect and disabling when its stack is empty; undoing a drag reorder restores the previous sibling order in the tree and compiled text

criteria.name: history_timeline_slider_scrubs
criteria.description: The history timeline slider spans the active schema's edit history, and dragging it scrubs the tree, compiled text, and example payload backward and forward through recorded states with the current position labeled by its action name

criteria.name: export_modal_live_and_copyable
criteria.description: The Export modal offers the compiled schema text, a SchemaPackage JSON preview, and, after a completed run, a validation report with the payload summary, per-field outcomes, and failure count; each format reflects the latest edits and run when reopened, and each Copy control puts the exact text on the clipboard with visible confirmation

criteria.name: boundary_states_handled
criteria.description: Deleting every child of the root yields a valid empty object schema (compiled text shows an object with no properties, the example shows an empty object), a sibling-duplicate rename shows a conflict message and is not applied, and an empty enum entry shows a message and is not added

criteria.name: schema_package_export_field_contract
criteria.description: Opening Export shows a live SchemaPackage JSON preview whose visible text includes schemaVersion exactly schema-package-v1 plus name, jsonSchema, fields, metadata, examplePayload, and formatInstruction; after adding a constrained field, reopening Export shows that field in fields and jsonSchema, and Copy and Download are available for the package format

criteria.name: schema_package_import_round_trip_and_reject
criteria.description: Exporting then importing a conforming SchemaPackage JSON reconstructs the same visible tree, compiled text, example payload, and format instruction; importing parseable JSON that fails the field contract (wrong schemaVersion, missing required keys, or a fields element outside the FieldDefinition contract) shows validation naming the offending field and leaves the working schema unchanged

criteria.name: empty_states_present
criteria.description: An emptied tree, an empty version list, an empty playground, and a deleted-schema state each render a designed empty state naming what belongs there, never a blank region

criteria.name: forms_validate_inline_before_submit
criteria.description: The FieldDefinition configuration constraints, Save version, metadata field builder, Import from example, and Import package validate inline before submit, with required-field and field-contract rule errors naming the field

criteria.name: errors_are_actionable
criteria.description: Error copy is actionable: the JSON parse error names the position, constraint errors name the FieldDefinition rule and bound, SchemaPackage import errors name schemaVersion or the offending key, and the rename-conflict message names the colliding sibling

criteria.name: actions_show_confirmation
criteria.description: Copying, saving a version, inserting into the prompt draft, applying an import, importing a package, and deleting each produce visible success feedback (toast or inline confirmation)

criteria.name: simulated_run_shows_progress
criteria.description: The simulated validation run shows live progress affordances — per-step statuses, the rollup, and the backoff countdown — rather than an unexplained wait

criteria.name: destructive_actions_guarded_and_undoable
criteria.description: Destructive actions are guarded and recoverable: node, bulk, and schema deletions require confirmation naming the scope, and tree deletions can be reverted via Undo

criteria.name: non_obvious_controls_have_help
criteria.description: Non-obvious controls — nest/un-nest, the history slider, constraint templates, import accept/reject — carry labels, tooltips, or hints that explain them in-app

criteria.name: controls_use_semantic_tags
criteria.description: Buttons, inputs, selects, checkboxes, and the slider use semantic interactive elements with real labels rather than bare div or span wrappers

criteria.name: overlays_support_close_paths
criteria.description: The export modal and confirmation dialogs close via their close control, Escape, and a background click where applicable

criteria.name: boundary_values_handled
criteria.description: Boundary inputs behave: a 40-plus-character field name truncates with an ellipsis in the tree and shows in full in the panel, an empty enum entry is rejected with a message, an empty-object schema stays valid, and undo/redo at empty stacks are disabled

criteria.name: schema_package_import_rejects_nonconforming
criteria.description: Importing parseable JSON that fails the SchemaPackage field contract — schemaVersion not exactly schema-package-v1, missing required name/jsonSchema/fields/metadata/examplePayload/formatInstruction keys, or a fields element outside the FieldDefinition contract — shows visible validation naming the offending field and leaves the working schema unchanged

criteria.name: spacing_and_sizing_follow_scale
criteria.description: Spacing and sizing follow a consistent design-system scale across the sidebar, tree, panel, and panes, with no arbitrary one-off values visible in computed styles

criteria.name: specified_treatments_implemented
criteria.description: The treatments the spec calls out exist as described: monospace type badges with per-type colors, the error-accent required asterisk, syntax-styled code surfaces, and the roughly 280 pixel docked configuration panel

criteria.name: layout_matches_specified_composition
criteria.description: The desktop layout matches the specified composition — library sidebar with metadata builder, central tree, and right tabbed panes for schema text, example, and format instruction

criteria.name: specified_state_changes_animate
criteria.description: The state changes the spec calls out animate at roughly their specified durations: 150 millisecond add expansion, 120 millisecond remove collapse, 200 millisecond reorder slide, panel slide, and step-ordered annotation pop-ins

criteria.name: responsive_behavior_matches_spec
criteria.description: Responsive behavior matches the spec: the sidebar collapses behind a toggle at 768 pixels and below, the panes stack as full-width tabs, and 375 pixels shows no page-level horizontal scroll

criteria.name: control_styling_cohesive
criteria.description: Buttons, tags, inputs, tabs, and form controls share cohesive radii, padding, and depth treatments rather than mixing unrelated styles

criteria.name: typography_has_clear_hierarchy
criteria.description: Typography distinguishes app title, panel headings, tree rows, and body text as specified, with monospace reserved for badges and code

criteria.name: component_states_match_spec
criteria.description: Default, hover, focus, disabled, and error states are all present and visually distinct on interactive controls, as specified

criteria.name: delightful_microinteractions
criteria.description: The app has unique, delightful microinteractions beyond the specified motion — for example badges that morph on type change or a satisfying drop settle

criteria.name: advanced_motion_mechanics
criteria.description: The app has advanced animation mechanics beyond the spec, such as coordinated tree choreography or animated diff reveals

criteria.name: guided_onboarding
criteria.description: The app has a guided onboarding or first-run explanation of the tree editing, validation, and versioning workflow

criteria.name: enhanced_interactive_graphics
criteria.description: The tree, diff, or validation surfaces gain extra interactive visualization beyond the required markers — for example a minimap of large trees or a coverage gauge

criteria.name: keyboard_shortcut_depth
criteria.description: The app supports a broader keyboard-shortcut surface than required (per-type add shortcuts, a shortcut reference overlay)

criteria.name: preference_personalization
criteria.description: The app provides personalization such as adjustable tree density, configurable default types, or pane arrangement preferences

criteria.name: polished_product_narrative
criteria.description: The app presents a polished, coherent product identity — naming, empty states, and copy feel like one designed product rather than assembled panels

criteria.name: dynamic_theming_beyond_requirements
criteria.description: The app provides a theme or color mode beyond requirements, applied consistently across all surfaces including the code panes

criteria.name: schema_intelligence_extras
criteria.description: The app offers schema intelligence beyond the spec — inline constraint documentation hints, a shareable one-line schema summary, suggested constraints from example values, or a short first-run coachmark for Export package

criteria.name: competition_level_innovation
criteria.description: Competition-level innovation is visible to users beyond everything already listed

criteria.name: innovation_catchall
criteria.description: The app demonstrates a noteworthy, browser-observable enhancement beyond the spec that is NOT covered by any other criterion in this file. The enhancement must plausibly matter to a real user, not be a nitpick. If present, name the enhancement and cite the concrete evidence (element, page state, screenshot) that demonstrates it. If the enhancement is already covered — even partially — by another criterion in this file, answer no here and let that criterion carry it.

criteria.name: headings_use_consistent_capitalization
criteria.description: Where the app renders headings and panel titles (library, tree, playground, versions, export), they use one consistent capitalization convention throughout

criteria.name: actions_use_specific_labels
criteria.description: Where the app renders action labels, they are specific verbs such as Add field, Save version, and Run validation rather than generic labels like Submit or OK when a specific label is possible

criteria.name: errors_name_problem_and_fix
criteria.description: Where the app renders FieldDefinition, Save version, or Import package validation messages, they name the field and the field-contract fix (for example allowed key pattern, type enum, minimum versus maximum, or schemaVersion schema-package-v1), not only a bare rejection like 'Invalid'

criteria.name: empty_states_explain_next_step
criteria.description: Where the app renders empty states (blank schema, no versions, empty playground), the copy explains what belongs there and how to add it

criteria.name: body_copy_is_well_written
criteria.description: Where the app renders descriptions, format instructions, and notices, rate how free the copy is of spelling and grammatical errors

criteria.name: terminology_is_consistent
criteria.description: Where the app names the same concept in multiple places, terminology is consistent — always fields (not properties here and keys there), the same type names, the same version and package terminology across the tree, panel, diff, and exports

criteria.name: numbers_dates_and_units_are_consistent
criteria.description: Where the app renders timestamps (versions, event timeline) and numeric bounds, formatting is consistent — one date format, consistent precision

criteria.name: success_messages_are_specific
criteria.description: Where the app renders confirmations, they state what happened — Schema copied, Version saved, Instruction inserted — not vague affirmations

criteria.name: edit_flow_reaches_every_pane
criteria.description: The core editing flow works end to end: adding, renaming, requiring, and constraining a field via a valid FieldDefinition lands in the tree, compiled text, example payload, format instruction, and SchemaPackage export within a beat of each edit and without a reload

criteria.name: invalid_forms_validate_inline
criteria.description: Submitting Save version with an empty name, entering min greater than max, an invalid pattern, a sibling-duplicate key, or a metadata field with no label triggers immediate inline validation naming the field and the FieldDefinition or VersionSnapshot rule and applies nothing

criteria.name: validation_flow_end_to_end
criteria.description: The validation flow works end to end: paste payload, run, watch steps advance, see annotations land on the tree, and read the same outcomes in the validation report export

criteria.name: delete_flow_updates_all_surfaces
criteria.description: Deleting a node (singly or via bulk delete) removes it and its descendants from the tree, compiled text, example payload, format instruction, and any annotations after deletion

criteria.name: schema_switch_retains_context
criteria.description: Switching between library schemas and back loads each schema's own tree, and switching right-pane tabs retains the underlying schema state — no switch silently loses edits already committed

criteria.name: empty_tree_state_recovers
criteria.description: Deleting the loaded schema, or emptying the root's children, shows a clear state (blank-schema message or empty-object output) with working controls to create or select a schema and continue

criteria.name: versioning_flow_complete
criteria.description: The versioning flow works end to end: save, edit, save again, select the two versions, read the color-coded diff, and return to editing without losing the working tree

criteria.name: collapsible_chrome_preserves_workflow
criteria.description: Collapsing and reopening the sidebar, the configuration panel, and tree branches preserves workflow continuity — the same schema, selection, and edits remain in place

criteria.name: overlays_support_expected_flows
criteria.description: The export modal, delete confirmations, and inline confirms open, complete their flow, and close as expected, returning the user to an intact editing context

criteria.name: flow_recovers_without_reload
criteria.description: Every dead-end candidate recovers in-app: an invalid pasted payload, a failed validation step, a rejected import draft, a rejected SchemaPackage import, and an emptied library all leave working controls to continue without a reload

criteria.name: export_package_flow_with_field_contract
criteria.description: Export package flow: edit the schema by adding a constrained field, open Export, confirm the SchemaPackage JSON preview shows schemaVersion schema-package-v1 plus name, jsonSchema, fields, metadata, examplePayload, and formatInstruction with the new field present, then Import package that JSON and confirm the tree, compiled text, example, and format instruction restore the same session work

criteria.name: controls_are_keyboard_accessible
criteria.description: Every interactive control — tree rows and their toggles, chevrons, the configuration inputs, tabs, version selectors, the history slider, and run controls — is reachable and operable with Tab, Shift+Tab, Enter, Space, or arrow keys

criteria.name: overlays_manage_focus
criteria.description: The export modal and confirmation dialogs trap focus while open, close on Escape, and return focus to the control that opened them

criteria.name: icons_have_accessible_names
criteria.description: Icon-only controls (delete, nest/un-nest, chevrons, copy) carry accessible names or labels; decorative icons are hidden from the accessibility tree

criteria.name: run_outcomes_announced
criteria.description: Completion of a validation run and a step entering the failed state are announced through an aria-live region as well as shown visually

criteria.name: forms_have_explicit_labels
criteria.description: The FieldDefinition configuration panel, Save version, metadata builder, Import from example, and Import package fields have visible labels, and validation messages are programmatically associated with their fields

criteria.name: headings_follow_logical_order
criteria.description: Panel and section headings follow a logical order with no skips (H1 for the app, H2 for panels, H3 within)

criteria.name: tree_semantics_exposed
criteria.description: The schema tree exposes hierarchy semantically (tree or equivalent list-with-level semantics) so nesting depth and expanded states are readable by assistive technology, and landmark structure or a skip path is present

criteria.name: text_and_controls_have_contrast
criteria.description: All text — type badges, required asterisks, diff treatments, code surfaces, annotations — meets sufficient color contrast against its background

criteria.name: semantic_html_roles_are_used
criteria.description: Interactive and structural elements use semantic roles — real buttons for actions, real tab semantics for the pane tabs, slider semantics with value for the history control

criteria.name: reduced_motion_is_respected
criteria.description: With prefers-reduced-motion set, height expansions, reorder slides, panel transitions, and annotation pop-ins are removed while all functionality remains
criteria.reasoning: Observed via playwright testing that with reduced-motion forced, duration is returned as 0.

criteria.name: cold_start_is_under_two_seconds
criteria.description: Cold start to an interactive tree editor is under 2 seconds on local render

criteria.name: console_is_clean
criteria.description: Browser devtools show no errors or warnings on load or while exercising editing, drag reorder, validation, versioning, import from example, SchemaPackage import, and export

criteria.name: derivations_feel_instant
criteria.description: The compiled text, example payload, and format instruction land within roughly 100 milliseconds of any edit with no perceptible lag

criteria.name: simulated_run_has_progress_indicators
criteria.description: The simulated validation run shows continuous progress (step statuses, rollup) rather than a frozen or blank interface

criteria.name: large_trees_stay_smooth
criteria.description: A schema grown to dozens of fields with deep nesting still edits, collapses, and reorders smoothly without perceived lag

criteria.name: ui_interactive_during_run
criteria.description: The UI remains fully interactive while a validation run advances — switching tabs, selecting nodes, and reading panes all work mid-run

criteria.name: animations_maintain_smooth_frame_rate
criteria.description: Height expansions, reorder slides, and panel transitions hold a smooth frame rate with no visible stutter

criteria.name: rapid_input_does_not_freeze
criteria.description: Rapid repeated input — fast renames, rapid history-slider scrubbing, quick tab switches, repeated undo/redo — never hangs or drops interactions

criteria.name: extended_sessions_avoid_resource_runaway
criteria.description: An extended session with many edits, runs, versions, and scrubs shows no memory runaway or progressive slowdown

criteria.name: three_region_layout_with_docked_panel
criteria.description: At desktop width the app composes a left sidebar (schema library and metadata builder), a central tree editor, and a right region with tabs for compiled text, example, and format instruction; the configuration panel docks to the right at roughly 280 pixels wide with a structured form layout

criteria.name: tree_rows_indented_with_chevrons
criteria.description: The schema tree renders as indented rows with chevron toggles on object and array nodes, and indentation depth visibly communicates nesting level

criteria.name: type_badges_monospace_consistent
criteria.description: Type badges render in a monospace face inside compact tags with one consistent color per type (string, number, boolean, object, array) everywhere that type appears

criteria.name: required_asterisk_error_accent
criteria.description: Required fields show an asterisk in the error-accent color after the field name in the tree, consistent with the compiled text's required listing

criteria.name: code_surfaces_syntax_styled
criteria.description: The compiled schema text, example payload, and SchemaPackage JSON preview render in monospaced, syntax-styled read-only code surfaces with visible structure indentation

criteria.name: diff_treatments_labeled
criteria.description: Diff treatments are unambiguous: added fields in the success color, removed in the error color, changed in the warning color, each carrying a text label so color is not the only signal

criteria.name: annotations_pair_icon_and_color
criteria.description: Validation annotations pair an icon with the color — a success mark on passing nodes and an error mark with message text on failing nodes — and step statuses (pending, running, retrying, failed, complete) are visually distinct and consistent between the step list and event timeline

criteria.name: typography_hierarchy
criteria.description: Typography shows a clear hierarchy — app title larger than panel headings, panel headings larger than tree row and body text — consistent across views

criteria.name: spacing_and_component_states
criteria.description: Spacing follows a consistent rhythm across the sidebar, tree, and panes with no crowded or orphaned regions, and buttons, inputs, selects, toggles, and checkboxes show distinct default, hover, focus (visible ring), disabled, and error treatments with one consistent icon set throughout

criteria.name: serves_clean_and_fast
criteria.description: The app serves via its documented npm start path on port 3000, renders a working interface (not a blank or broken page), and is interactive within 2 seconds of a local cold load

criteria.name: shared_state_coherence
criteria.description: A single edit propagates everywhere at once: renaming a field updates the tree, compiled text, example payload, format instruction, and SchemaPackage export together — the same datum never shows different values in two panes without a reload

criteria.name: reload_resets_to_seed
criteria.description: A page reload returns the app to its seeded baseline: the seeded schema library with the first schema active, empty undo history, no user versions, and an empty playground

criteria.name: console_stays_clean
criteria.description: No console errors or unhandled promise rejections appear on load or during a full exercise of editing, drag reorder, validation runs, versioning, import from example, SchemaPackage import, undo/redo scrubbing, and exports

criteria.name: compiled_output_parses_as_json
criteria.description: The compiled schema text and the SchemaPackage JSON preview are valid JSON at all times — after any sequence of edits the package parses without error, carries schemaVersion schema-package-v1, and its jsonSchema carries the draft-07 $schema declaration and standard keywords

criteria.name: rapid_interaction_stays_synced
criteria.description: Rapid interleaved interaction — fast renames, quick history-slider scrubs, tab switches during a validation run — leaves all panes consistent with each other, with no desynced or stale derivations

criteria.name: api_shaped_exports_round_trip
criteria.description: SchemaPackage export and Import package use the same field contract: a package exported from the session can be re-imported to reconstruct the same tree and derived panes, and browser storage remains unused for that persistence

criteria.name: layout_adapts_desktop_to_mobile
criteria.description: The sidebar-tree-panes layout adapts gracefully from 1440px desktop to 375px mobile without breaking the editor, playground, or version surfaces

criteria.name: mobile_tap_targets_are_large_enough
criteria.description: At mobile width, tree-row toggles, chevrons, checkboxes, and tab headers are tap targets of at least 44px

criteria.name: typography_resizes_across_breakpoints
criteria.description: Tree rows, badges, code surfaces, and body text resize appropriately between desktop and mobile and remain readable at both

criteria.name: content_avoids_clipping_and_overflow
criteria.description: No tree row, panel, tab strip, or code surface clips or overflows the viewport at any width between 1440px and 375px

criteria.name: chrome_adapts_below_768
criteria.description: At widths of 768 pixels and below the sidebar collapses behind a toggle that opens it as an overlay and the right panes stack below the tree as full-width tabs; at desktop widths all three regions are visible together

criteria.name: stacking_reflows_logically
criteria.description: At narrow widths the regions stack in a logical order — tree first, then the panes — with the configuration panel reachable as an overlay rather than interleaving arbitrarily

criteria.name: deep_nesting_stays_usable
criteria.description: Deeply nested tree levels remain readable and operable at narrow widths — indentation compresses or scrolls within the tree container rather than pushing rows off screen

criteria.name: small_screens_avoid_horizontal_scroll
criteria.description: At 375px width there is no page-level horizontal scrolling anywhere in the app

criteria.name: code_surfaces_scroll_contained
criteria.description: The compiled text, example payload, and report surfaces scroll within their own containers at every width

criteria.name: fixed_controls_remain_accessible
criteria.description: Undo/redo, the history slider, and the run controls remain reachable at every viewport size

criteria.name: add_field_height_expand
criteria.description: Adding a field through the real Add Field control animates the new row in with a height expansion of roughly 150 milliseconds rather than appearing instantly

criteria.name: remove_field_height_collapse
criteria.description: Removing a field through the real Delete flow collapses its height over roughly 120 milliseconds before it disappears

criteria.name: drag_reorder_slides_siblings
criteria.description: Drag reordering with the real pointer slides sibling nodes into their new positions over roughly 200 milliseconds rather than snapping

criteria.name: panel_slide_and_tab_crossfade
criteria.description: The configuration panel slides in when a node is selected and out when dismissed, and switching between the schema text, example, and format instruction tabs crossfades briefly

criteria.name: annotations_pop_in_step_order
criteria.description: During a validation run started from the real Run validation control, tree annotations appear with a short pop-in in step order as each field's step completes, not all at once

criteria.name: countdown_ticks_and_status_fades
criteria.description: The retry backoff countdown ticks visibly and a completing step's status transitions with a short fade rather than snapping

criteria.name: history_slider_scrubs_continuously
criteria.description: Dragging the history timeline slider updates the tree continuously as it moves, not only on release

criteria.name: hover_system_required
criteria.description: While hovering with the real pointer: buttons ease background and shadow with a slight press effect on activation, tree rows, library entries, and version rows take a full-width hover wash, and form controls show focus rings — verified via computed styles while hovering

criteria.name: toasts_slide_and_dismiss
criteria.description: Toasts for copies, saves, inserts, and deletions slide in, remain readable, and auto-dismiss with a fade

criteria.name: reduced_motion_complete
criteria.description: With prefers-reduced-motion set, expansions, collapses, reorders, tab switches, and annotations apply instantly while every feature remains fully usable

criteria.name: multi_facet_reload_reset
criteria.description: Add a field, save a version, define a metadata field, paste a playground payload, and switch the right pane to the Example tab — then reload the page. All facets coherently reset to the seeded baseline: the seeded library with the first schema active, no user versions or metadata fields, an empty playground, and empty undo history — never a mix of surviving and reset facets

criteria.name: edit_to_all_derivations_pipeline
criteria.description: Full pipeline probe: add a number field with a FieldDefinition key matching the allowed pattern, minimum 1 and maximum 5, mark it required, then confirm without a reload that the tree shows it, the compiled text gains its property with minimum, maximum, and the required entry, the example payload shows it with a value between 1 and 5, the format instruction names it as a required number, and the SchemaPackage export fields/jsonSchema contain that same field — all surfaces track the one edit

criteria.name: validate_edit_revalidate_tracks
criteria.description: Paste a payload violating one constraint, run validation, and confirm exactly that field fails with a message naming the path and bound; edit the schema so the constraint now passes, re-run validation on the same payload, and confirm the annotation flips to pass and the exported validation report reflects the new outcome

criteria.name: different_payloads_different_outcomes
criteria.description: Validate two different payloads against the same schema: the per-field annotations, rollup failure counts, and validation reports differ in ways the payloads dictate

criteria.name: version_diff_matches_edits_exactly
criteria.description: Save a version, make exactly two structural edits (one added field, one type change), save a second version, and diff the two: the diff shows exactly one added entry and one changed entry with correct labels, and nothing else

criteria.name: import_inference_input_dependent
criteria.description: Paste two different example objects into Import from example in sequence: each inferred draft's field names and types match its own pasted object, and applying the second draft produces a tree, compiled text, and example payload matching the second object's shape

criteria.name: interleaved_run_and_edit_integrity
criteria.description: Start a validation run, pause it mid-run, switch tabs and edit an unrelated schema field's description, then resume: the run continues from exactly the paused step with earlier outcomes unchanged, and the description edit is still present — neither flow corrupted the other

criteria.name: history_scrub_round_trip
criteria.description: Make three labeled edits, scrub the history slider back to before the first edit and confirm the tree, compiled text, and example match the pre-edit state, then scrub forward to the end and confirm all three edits are back — derived panes track through both directions

criteria.name: export_import_pipeline_preserves_session_contract
criteria.description: Full pipeline probe: add a constrained field, open Export and confirm the SchemaPackage JSON shows schemaVersion schema-package-v1 with that field in fields and jsonSchema, copy or download the package, Import package it into a blank or different schema, and confirm the tree, compiled text, example payload, and format instruction match the exported session — then Import package a payload with schemaVersion not schema-package-v1 and confirm the tree is unchanged
